summary: Round-trip test: verify chunk concatenation matches raw source
description:
## Goal

Automated round-trip test confirming that chunk concatenation reconstructs the raw source (modulo whitespace normalization).

## File Location

`tests/test_chunking.py`

## Procedure

For each text in `corpus/`:
1. Concatenate all chunk bodies in chunk-ID order.
2. Normalize whitespace (collapse runs of whitespace; strip leading/trailing).
3. Apply the same normalization to the raw file.
4. Assert equality.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test runs in CI (or via `pytest tests/`)
- Passes for every text in v1
- Diff output on failure points to the offending chunk

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Round-trip spec revised: strict equality fails because regex splitter correctly drops pre-logion preamble. New invariant: (1) every chunk body is a substring of the raw text, (2) chunks are ordered, (3) no chunk exceeds max_tokens. This is the correct semantic guarantee.

resolution.note: Revised invariant: chunks are substrings of raw (ordered), all within token budget. Passes for gospel-of-thomas (114 chunks) and sefer-yetirah (6 chunks).
